meaning: n.厚木板,支架,(政党的)政纲条款vt.铺板,立刻付款vi.睡在板上
examples: She sawed the plank in half.
A plank of wood bridged the stream.
The plank was spliced with nails.
His chief plank, a plan to streamline the government bureaucracy, got a 97% vote.
Targeting inflation has been a key plank of economic orthodoxy around the world for decades.
